# WI-QUIC-CRT-0042 - QUIC client-side successor 1-RTT Key Phase install work item

## Summary

Add successor 1-RTT open/protect material install on the first observed client Key Phase transition, keep establishing runtimes unchanged before handshake confirmation, and keep general TLS KeyUpdate, repeated successor derivation, transfer/retry, and public API widening out of scope.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0145

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0040

## Planned Changes

- Retain the current 1-RTT application traffic secret material long enough to derive successor open/protect material on the first observed 0->1 Key Phase transition.
- Reuse the existing bridge-state install path so the successor pair overwrites the active 1-RTT open/protect view and the key-phase bit flips to 1 only after the first observed transition.
- Update the active short-header send path to emit the installed phase bit for subsequent outbound 1-RTT packets.
- Keep establishing runtimes unchanged before handshake confirmation.
- Keep repeated key-update derivation, TLS KeyUpdate support, transfer/retry, public API widening, and any general RFC 9001 key-update engine out of scope.

## Out of Scope

- general RFC 9001 key-update engine
- TLS KeyUpdate support
- repeated successor derivation
- transfer enablement
- retry enablement
- public API widening
- public key-update promises
- 0-RTT receive handling
- broader stream-management parity
- opportunistic refactors unrelated to the successor install boundary

## Verification Plan

Run focused requirement-home tests for positive successor install, negative establishing-runtime no-install, negative duplicate same-phase no-op, outbound phase-bit propagation, and the public no-broad-support guard; add fuzz coverage for the short-header Key Phase boundary; run the permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite for the successor path; then run the current CRT/API/INT guard set, the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep, and SpecTrace render/validation checks.
